methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $52M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Medra is a Series A biotech & health company founded by Michelle Lee that has raised $52M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers (including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, and Asimov) at Series B stage, valued between $100M and $3.3B, as well as 3 public sector peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two quantitative methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach uses the $52M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $104M. The peer-median method anchors to the 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $805M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue data for the target. The peer range spans $100M to $3.3B, reflecting meaningful dispersion in the cohort.

The triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $88M (floor) to $926M (ceiling), with a median estimate of $454M. The wide range reflects the stage and capital intensity of biotech ventures, where outcomes remain materially uncertain at Series A. Key uncertainties include the target's technology maturity, clinical-stage progress, and intellectual property position relative to the peer cohort. The median estimate sits well below the peer median, appropriate given Medra's earlier stage versus the predominantly Series B peer set.
